criteria.description: GraphNeighbors returns a source fileID's neighbors from the compacted CSR graph.dat
0-2 hop traversal semantics: depth=0 empty, depth=1 direct only, depth=2 expands one more hop, never beyond depth
Traversal respects an edge-type filter built on 3.1.4's ValidEdgeType/EdgeTypeName/ParseEdgeType
Result size hard-capped at maxNodes when reachable-node count exceeds it
criteria.check: engine/graph/traverse_test.go:TestGraphNeighbors/CapExactlyN, /CapNPlus1, /CapZero, /LargeGraphExceedsCapWithin2Hops (literal issue test-spec wording: >maxNodes reachable within 2 hops)

criteria.description: A node reachable via multiple BFS paths is never double-counted
Invalid depth (<0 or >2), invalid edgeTypeFilter (undefined byte), negative maxNodes all rejected with an error rather than silently misbehaving

GraphNeighbors reads only the compacted CSRGraph (no EdgeLog merge) - documented design decision, not directly testable as an absence, verified by code review of traverse.go's signature/body (takes *CSRGraph, no *EdgeLog parameter)
criteria.check: engine/graph/traverse.go signature inspection + requirement.md design-question section

criteria.description: Full package suite remains green after adding traverse.go
